title: Departing-Insider Data Exfiltration
description: Response playbook for a departing or disgruntled insider exfiltrating sensitive data ahead of resignation or termination. Authorized investigation only, conducted with HR and Legal. Triggered by a DLP alert, an HR notice of a high-risk departure, or anomalous bulk downloads to removable media or personal cloud storage.
author: Cognis Digital

steps.name: Align an HR/Legal-coordinated process
steps.description: Maintain a documented, privacy-respecting process for high-risk-departure reviews that is run jointly with HR and Legal under defined authorization.

steps.name: Ensure DLP and access visibility
steps.description: Confirm DLP coverage for email, endpoint, removable media, and sanctioned cloud apps, and that access to sensitive repositories is logged.

steps.name: Confirm anomalous data movement
steps.description: Validate the alert: unusual bulk access or copying of sensitive files relative to the user's normal role and recent baseline.
steps.detection: DLP and access logs for spikes in downloads from document stores, mass file opens, or access to repositories outside the user's normal scope.

steps.name: Identify the exfiltration channel
steps.description: Determine how data is leaving: removable media, personal webmail, or unsanctioned cloud sync.
steps.detection: Endpoint logs for USB mass-storage writes, uploads to personal cloud or webmail, and large outbound transfers to non-corporate destinations.

steps.name: Constrain access proportionately
steps.response: With HR/Legal approval, reduce the user's access to sensitive data, block the identified exfiltration channel (e.g. disable USB write, block the personal-cloud domain), and preserve evidence.

steps.name: Preserve evidence and chain of custody
steps.response: Capture endpoint, DLP, and access logs and any media images under documented chain of custody before the user departs or is notified.

steps.name: Revoke access on separation
steps.response: On the agreed timeline, disable accounts, revoke tokens and device access, and recover corporate devices and removable media.

steps.name: Assess impact and remediate exposure
steps.response: Determine what data left, whether notification obligations apply, and remediate exposure (e.g. rotate any secrets in copied files) with Legal guidance.

steps.name: Improve the departure-risk program
steps.description: Refine DLP rules for the observed channel, tighten just-in-time access for sensitive stores, and review the offboarding checklist with HR.
